channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever! — 31 replies — 75.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT ALWAYS GOES UP! Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:54 UTC

OP

IT ALWAYS GOES UP!
Bobo is temporary, Mumu is forever!

Early replies (first 3)

62283021
>>62283018
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283022
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

62283030
>please buy my overpriced bags before the market crashes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283037
>>62283018
that's a really good point. I just buyed

62283047
now show other market index's like the FSTE 100, is it even above 2008 levels yet????

62283203
>>62283039
ONE nothing wrong with me
TWO

62283258
It's always misleading as fuck
>Your current holdings get rekt by 90%
>It's okay though the stuff you buy from this point onwards is cheap and will grow in recovery
>But what about everything I held before?
>Still rekt for years on end
>We cope by calling that "reducing your average buy in"
Not falling for it personally

62283283
>>62283018
Objective truth. Never doom.

62283382
so why should we be immune from a Japan style 30 year stagnation? We had one before 1960. Allowing retards like Trump in power is begging for it.

62283411
>>62283382
Then buy Chinese or Euro stocks, most of which have already been sideways for ~2 decades.

62283417
>>62283382
Japan largely stopped being innovative desu, not America’s case so far

62283444
>>62283411
Many famous Euro stocks are actually down 30 or 40% since 2000 lol (Mercedes-Benz, Shell, Deutsche Bank, Philips, Bouygues, UniCredit, Veolia…)

62283452
>>62283185
So you need to have over 30m now to have what a millionaire had 1900. That's why millionaire was really special back then. So a million now is not even 30k in 1900.

62283465
>>62283444
I'm also amazed at some Chinese ones. Vanke used to be the flagship of Chinese stock market and the most prestigious real estate developer. The ticker number for it was chosen to be 000002, after 000001 being the Shanghai Composite Index

62283472
>>62283452
That distinction still exists, we call them UHNWIs nowadays

62283495
>>62283465
>Vanke
Looks like a crypto after the rug pull, almost as bad as Vivendi and Orange (the absolute juggernauts of the French stock market in 2000, they were supposed to crush the world with all their ambitious tech… 26 years later, still 90% down after the bubble burst…)

62283519
>>62283018
So they're just flat-out admitting that inflation has been 10% every year since 1950?

62283598
>>62283039
Is this inflation adjusted?

62283626
>>62283598
Dividing SPX by the USM2 is almost the same thing as adjusting to inflation.

62283790
>>62283411
How do you know which stock market might be the next Nikkei? It went up, until it didn't, and then took 35 years to recover

62283830
>>62283790
>which stock market might be the next Nikkei?
All of them

62284284
>>62283018
>just wait like 5 years goy, don't sell, it'll return to green eventually

62284502
>>62284284
>the twin towers of anon's portfolio

62284646
just buy and hold for 45 years, that's my motto

62284879
>>62284502
kek and they're right next to "september 11", I swear it was unintentional

62284961
>>62284646
>DUDE JUST WORK AND DEAL IN THE REAL WORLD FOR 45 YEARS

do goyim really?

62285025
>>62283021
>>62284284
>>62284646
whats the alternative?
>inb4 just buy gold

62286115
>>62284502
Kek

>>62285025
The market can keep on being exuberant for many months, so keep more and more cash by your side, a play that’s too contrarian like leveraged shorts could wipe you

item.title: Orders Thread — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Orders Thread

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-28 22:18 UTC

OP

Post your orders
I think XLM may be nearly overvalued.

Early replies (first 3)

62285003
>>62284993
pic related trading view paper trading

62285235
>>62284993
the order was filled

62285253
>>62285235
Its filled on paper, try to fill it in real life goy, where cz can scamwick the shit out of you ten times a day....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285267
>>62285253
you have been posting this image frequently

62285273
>>62285267
And i am sexy, now thats two facts, what now?

62285278
>>62285253
the bills in your image are also filled on paper and are made of cotton and paper.
designing fishing rods is higher yield than selling fish at the market

62285282
>>62285273
2 more facts, a total of 4 facts

62285299
>>62284993
garbage coin to time trades on unless you want to catch a scamwick
zero structure and no respect for any levels, just unpredictable spikes that could range anywhere from 20% to 200% based on news

62285312
>>62285299
I agree that this coin is highly unpredictable.
I'm comfortable with my estimated risk/reward and the position.
The support was not respected a few weeks/days ago, but unrespected support would suggest weakness, and weakness would suggest a respect of resistance.

62285329
>>62284993
The stop loss has been moved to breakeven
I am going out for a walk to apply for a job as a full time line cook at a nearby restaurant.
Thankyou all for your attention to this post.

62286172
YES retards sell now into this pump; its not like the DTCC, the backbone of capital markets is going to tokenize on stellar.

62286196
>>62286172
Bullish for Chainlink

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-28 22:05 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285966
>>62285713
TP? Where are you? And do you still own BAT?

62286043
>>62285619
bat og here. im heavily underwater with my bat investment at this point but still holding. i'm never going to sell - not because bat will be some kind of currency in the future and i won't need to sell it like some link cope, but I don't want to make profit from BAT anymore and I also don't want anyone to use my tokens for ill purposes. truth be told, after reading that brendan eich in 2006 didn't think that people with xy chromosomes that undergo hrt and srs maybe shouldn't be allowed to compete in female chess tournaments has really had me rethinking my investment and the morality of it. i only want to invest in ethical companies like lockheed martin that actually help make democracy more accessible to global citizens around the world. if over 20 years ago the ceo of brave browser had such a controversial view it makes me concerned that he might have some problematic ideas today. did he even get the covid vaccine? im not sure, and I don't want to find out the hard way. i will simply hold onto my hundreds of thousands of bat, and not sell them, not lend them, just freeze them up on my hard drive in a password i refuse to commit to memory and dispose of my thinkpad into landfill. i encourage all other bat holders with a conscience to do the same.

62286053
>>62286043
Wow. That's some deep pasta bro. Missed this one

62286063
>>62285713
>IT'S BEEN A WHILE
It's been a hot minute. Sup, lad?

>>62285966
>Where are you?
o/
>do you still own BAT?
Of course, lad *sobs *

Haven't seen a thread in a minute. Was planning on doing some thot bait threads when we get a new JanJan.

62286087
>>62286063
>Blue hair
Gross

62286122
>>62286087
I mean if you want me to post men or trannies for you, just give me the word ^_^
_

Anyone else getting some pretty gnarly memory leaks from Brave recently?

62286148
>>62286122
This is better. Thanks. Looks like a boobier version of that Claire Manson fashion bitch on instagram. Thanks TP

62286153
>>62286063
>>62286122
Hawt
Got any uber thicc goths??

item.title: This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle. — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-28 22:12 UTC

OP

This is the only crypto that brings me any excitement I think it's poised to be the ETH/SOL/BNB of the next cycle.

Early replies (first 3)

62285843
>eth/sol/bnb
I hope niggy 10xes from here. Not because it would make me rich, but because of all the other shit around it that would pump.

62285851
>>62285810
A non-LINK project? On the LINK board?
They use their own oracles as well?
AND it's only gone up since?

No no no this will not do. Jannies send this nigger to the shadow realm

62285856
>>62285810
buy a fucking ad.
CLEAN IT UP JANNIES.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285858
>>62285856
Kek there he is

62285933
>>62285856
you've fed sergey enough big macs dont you think? time to give it a rest

62285953
>>62285810
>edgex enters the chat

62285995
>>62285810
Some anon pointed out that it feels like a new meta for alts. Kind of had to retruth that

62286128
>>62285995
it's been clear as day this has been the case for a while now as long as you weren't afflicted with bag bias from holding some of these absolutely raped alts from previous cycles with a team/VC unlock schedule that looks like a crash game.
It just sucks that it took a perp dex to finally align itself fully with its baggies but oh well.

Trump also made a Truth post yesterday explicitly mentioning
>Crypto Perpetuals
>Offshore company
>Bringing them back to the US
And guess which company fits all 3. I'm actually cautiously optimistic this orange fucker maybe kingmakes Hyperliquid but realistically he's probably going to do some scummy shit with WLFI to extract even more from his goyim base

62286151
>>62286128
I hope Trump doesn't touch HYPE. Everything that orange faggot and his family touches turns to ash. I don't want to be an employim anymore

62286180
ah there it is just confirms it.

item.title: I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino — 34 replies — 36.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
I won about 8 LTC from a crypto casino

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:22 UTC

OP

I have 15 in total now.. I am gonna hodl them.

Early replies (first 3)

62270209
>>62270028
Swap for XMR and hodl

62270220
>>62270209
Thats my plan but Monero's price is just too high right now. So, I will just wait and see for now. LTC is near its this years low, so it makes sense holding it or even buying more of it.

62271797
aim for 50 at least, this coin is going to power the decentralized AI agent economy

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62272140
>>62270028
I have 800

62272349
no one mentions MWEB which is one solid reason to be bullish on LTC

62272353
>>62272349
qrd?
Also what is the make it and sui stack for this?

62272539
>>62272353
It's a lightweight privacy extension, essentially. It hides the amount and receiver. It doesn't hide the sender though (though there are some tools like Hector Chu's coinjoin that would essentially do this if people used it.) Make it stack on LTC has always been 84. Same as having 21 BTC since it has a maximum supply of 84 million.

As for SUI, be very careful of VC coins people shill. Lots of insiders staking the premine waiting to dump it. I'm not saying don't buy it, just be aware of that. There will come a day when people that got it for free (including people marketing it, which is probably how you heard about it someone marketing it getting paid in the coin and not disclosing they got a shitload of them for free). Not saying they can't pump, but they can dump very hard and never recover. Fun to swing trade though.

62272587
>>62272539
How many LTC or XMR do you have sensei?

62272618
>>62272587
I stopped buying XMR when it went to $300. It could potentially be the most valuable coin, but it has so many liquidity channels cut off I'm happy with where I'm at. You should probably treat the make it stack as 21.

Both the qubic event in XMR and inflation bug in LTC (even though it was caught and didn't do permanent damage) have made me weary on privacy coins. I think you should have them, but BTC should be your core stack if you're new. If I were just entering the market, I'd probably pick up an LTC stack first since it's so cheap right now, then an XMR stack. Not necessarily a make it stack, but just something so you don't FOMO if they pump. Since BTC.d is so high, it'd pick up the alts for maybe a year (just so you don't fomo them later) and then never buy them again and focus on accumulating BTC at any price. I might be wrong and BTC just accelerates higher, but I don't think it'll go so high so fast it'll make much of a difference if you pick up the alts first. 1st generation PoW is pretty ignored right now except BTC, so they are probably safe bets. Of course I'll keep my on stack amounts a secret. Nothing good can come from telling anyone.

62272639
>>62272618
Thanks, I appreciate the advice :)

62272654
>>62272618
do you think xmr could realistically do a huge X from where it is?

62272668
>>62272654
Yeah. BTC ran to 20k in 2017 on retail fomo essentially. There has been a huge increase in the global money supply since then. Honestly, BTC could go to like 700k and it wouldn't surprise me, XMR could go to 4k and it wouldn't surprise me and LTC could go to $800 and it wouldn't surprise me. All this shit is undervalued. Another thing is fiat always dies. All fiat currencies have died historically and the governments of the world are doing the same thing that has killed all fiat currencies historically. It's just a matter of when. In the mean time though, we still have to live in a world where we settle in fiat, so don't invest more than you can afford to lose. Just treat these things as extra savings accounts.

62272838
>>62271797
BULLSHIT

62272845
>>62272539
No one said a fucking thing about sui retarded nigger he asked what a suicide stack was.

62272863
>>62271814
Who cares? Swap vs keep. It’s still a lose-lose bet in all altcoins atp, even Ai agrees lmao

62272971
>>62272845
It specifically says sui you dumb nigger. Not that guys fault you guys speak in nigger babble slang rather than typing it out.

62273235
>>62272863
Yeah Bitcoin is the only way to not loose money, why even have a bank account just keep all your life savings in bitcoin, since it's so stable and basically a guaranteed profite. You go first anon.

62273441
>>62270220
>Monero's price is just too high right now
Sub 300 is gone forever

62273491
>>62272971
you must be over 18 to post on this site

>>62273235
everything’s fake and gay anyway

62273626
>>62273235
Bitcoin is the only crypto club where there’s a long waiting list to get in and you’ll never get turned away because you didn’t follow the dress code that only changed because your ugly ass showed up or even if the club is filled to capacity

62275417
>>62273626
The fuck are you even trying to say?

62275720
>>62272838
enjoy being poor and destitute when the economy is completely overtaken by AI systems and you are relegated to the permanent bottom feeder caste

62276284
>>62275720
LTC WILL NEVER BE CONSIDERED FOR AI YOU INSANE TROON

62278357
>>62276284
sure whatever you say, in 5 years or so your only income stream will come from sucking troon cock anyway

when they'll pay you 0.0002 ltc for each cock sucking session, you'll think back to this thread

62281080
>>62278357
YOU WILL CRY WHEN LTC GOES TO 0.01 LOL LMAO WHAT A CLOWN

62282347
>>62275417
I’m saying you’re a retard with maybe 1/7th knowledge of how bitcoin actually works

62282992
>>62281080
you'll cry after each cock sucking session yourself, regardless of the price of ltc

62283412
LTC CHADS EMERGENCY GATHERING HOW DO WE COPE WITH THE IMMINENT DUMP TO 30$

62283733
>>62283412
I'd buy a bunch at $30

62284587
>>62283733
b b b based i guess
i'll be to mind broken to slurp above 20$

62284798
>>62283412
Doesn't look dumping much.

62285955
>>62284798
yeah, we good for today.
i'm scared we go to 0, which means you should buy more. I am a reliable bottom signal

62285994
LOOKS LIKE LTC IS COOKED LOL

item.title: Day 13 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000 — 18 replies — 18.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 13 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:20 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62284484
>>62283370
.

62284489
Please fuck my ass

62284919
>>62284489
you say that every day, creep.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62284922
>>62283370
ICP is finished OP and I say that as a cucked ICP holder. All their technical people are leaving and the price chart will forever look like the euthanasia roller coaster until the price hits 3 cents. They had an opportunity to become THE AI chain and totally fumbled the opportunity with mismanagement. I have no faith in Dominic's strategic direction at this point. I'm dissolving my 6month neuron with the hopes to sell by EOY to write off a loss for tax purposes.

62284936
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62284942
>>62284922
ICP has a partnership with the government of Pakistan, Switzerland and UN. Also has a partnership with HBAR, Maestro and Elon Musk’s brother is one of the holders.
ICP has one of the fastest growing developers and more thanks to Caffeine AI.
Some shit coins like kaspa only have 5 active devs while Dfinity has 200 + and thousands of people building every day.

The price in 2021 was manipulated, you are a cocksuckers.

62284948
>>62284922
>ICP is finished OP

* you are a cocksucker

62284992
>>62284942
>ICP has a partnership with the government of Pakistan

62284996
>>62284922
When you sell because of disinformation, just don’t kill yourself when ICP does a 100x.

62285005
>>62284992
80 million people is a joke to you? At least it’s being utilized by someone unlike 99% of snakeoil out there like Solana that don’t do anything besides memes.

62285370
>>62284992
.

62285887
>>62284992
.

62285944
>>62284942
>thousands of people building every day
This is just a plain lie. Nobody wants to build with gay Motoko. Andreas Rossberg left Dfinity 4 years ago now, and CTO Jan left last year. It's over for ICP's development. Single-line commits on github don't count worth shit.
>>62284996
Selling before it goes to $0.03

62285947
>>62284922
none of that matters for the price to go up

62285963
>>62285944
The chart doesn’t lie. Not only are people building on ICP but Caffeine made it possible for thousands of projects to be stored on the internet computer web service.

eat shit

62285965
>>62285944
cope

62285982
>>62285963
>>62285965
TOPKEK
so desperate for any attention

62285988
>>62285963
Caffeine is a grift scam by Lomesh the scammer and Dom, after they realized their multi-million dollar throwaway project HotOrNot (now YRAL) is a failure.

item.title: Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now. — 16 replies — 17.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:23 UTC

OP

Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

I bought a bunch of duplexes down south back around 2018-2020 because the numbers made sense. Cheap property taxes, cheap insurance, cheap properties in general. One duplex I paid $124k for is bringing in around $1925/mo total now and the tennants renewed instantly after the last increase.

Had a tennant last week who submitted a maintenence request because the microwave “stopped heating.” Property manager drove over there and the door wasnt shut all the way.

Another guy called because his screen door came off the track. PM slid it back in place in about 10 seconds and the guy goes “much obliged sir” like he just repaired farm equipment from the 1800s or something.

Whats weird is the older tennants almost never bother anybody. Pay on time. Maybe ask for a sink fix every few years. Younger ones send paragraphs over tiny stuff.

Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.

People online act like owning rentals is some impossible genius level thing but honestly half the job is just dealing with grown adults who dont know why the wifi stopped working after they unplugged the router to move furniture around.

Early replies (first 3)

62283853
>>62283816
It never sounds worth the money

62283860
How much of your revenue do you devote to hookers

62283871
>>62283816
I will buy one of these when I make it from Link. Probably in a college town. I won’t care about any profits but negotiate BJ’s for rent if they are unable to pay

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283878
>>62283816

Like this post, what exactly do you contribute?

62283893
>paying a property manager when you're only making 2k a month
i got sick of landlording. Every single time a tentant text me I was filled with dread and could never be bothered doing anything about the problems. You pay a shit load of taxes on it here too, not worth

62283964
>>62283816
>Live in a disposable consumerist society
>shocked when people don't know how to fix shit
Only reason I learned to fix shit was because I was too poor to replace. Now that I'm doing better, I still fix my shit because my money came from blood sweat and tears and I'll be damned if it goes to some fucking company so a CEO can take in more profit and his kids can go to Europe on a whim

62284183
>>62283816
so what you're telling me is owning a rental is like running daycare
yeah that sounds shit

62284244
>>62283878
It´s a subtle demotivation thread.

62284260
>>62283853
>>62284183
Yeah I feel like it should only ever be something temporary.
If you've got the money and brains to be a landlord you've got the money and brains to run a small convention center or camping ground at much less stress cause you can operate on predicable cycles.
I think the only thing more retarded than being a landlord long-term is deciding to run a cinema.

62284369
>>62283878
the property

62284379
>>62284369
lol

62284669
>>62283816
I rent a house and rarely ask for help/repairs because my landlord is a nice old widow, but to be honest if I didn't like my landlord I'd do it a lot more often, even on little things.

I do make it a point to not invest too much time or money in upkeep of the house, since I don't own it. That's throwing money away. Landlords owe it to tenants (and themselves!) to keep the place nice. So if a tenant wants some little thing fixed, even if it's not actually broken, it really should be the landlord's duty to fix it. It's one of the perks of renting, honestly.

62284712
>>62283816
Also, a $124K duplex earning $1925/mo - so that will take like ~6 years (probably closer to 7-10 years considering the costs of fixing things up, hiring property mgmt, taxes, possible vacancies, etc.) to pay off before you're earning pure profit.

That actually doesn't sound too bad as long as you have a real job bringing in real income the whole time

62284771
>>62283816
I lived at this one building and pipes were getting clogged every month. It was a horrible experience for everyone, tenants, maintenance guys and the pm. Multiple other issues like AC breaking down or electrical issues. It was cheap but i had to leave.

62284898
>>62283816
As long as you keep the place clean and safe I really don't give a fuck what you do. The problem is most landlords don't keep their properties clean and safe.

62285996
>>62283853
standard rental property will make money but sellers want to sell their properies at a 4-5% cap rate and its not really worth buying one unless you get a 10%+ cap rate. youd be better off buying into a REIT if you want real estate exposure

item.title: ICP has superior technology — 10 replies — 16.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP has superior technology

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:42 UTC

OP

All crypto today is fucking snakeoil except ICP. That’s why they manipulated the launch price with futures and tried to kill Dom’s project. Amazon and google are afraid of real competition.

Early replies (first 3)

62285029
>>62285022
Solana is a scam.

The only legit alts out there are ICP and their partner HBAR. You can make an argument for XRP Hyperliquid and link

62285041
>>62285022
imagine if this shit somehow end up mooning and i didn't buy any despite your endless shilling while it was 2$

62285042
>6 month old fogo has 3x the transactions of 11 year old eth
true and real

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285065
Betamax has superior picture quality than VHS. Betamax will be the future.

62285158
>>62285041
Yeah it's going to explode, it's going to go to $0.60 and then back up to $2.20 and the board will be full of euphoric ICP bots.

62285239
Utility and token price are not directly connected and when there is a vast amount of premined tokens in reserve just waiting to be dumped its in the companies best interest to suppress the price so that there is higher utilization.

62285307
>INTERNET COMPUTER


Cycles / s
33,667,085,111
+

Network market cap
$1,565,741,556
+
§Internet Computer Protocol (ICP)
Sovereign internet cloud for AI that builds
Tamperproof · Always-on · Yours

The Internet Computer is a frontier cloud created by a mathematically secure network protocol that combines independently operated compute nodes. Hosted apps and services are immune to infrastructure hacks, and guaranteed to run, so AI agents can build and update them hands-free, without the need for human security and systems teams in the loop. Frontier serverless languages introduce "orthogonal persistence," which enables agents to build sophisticated backends faster, using fewer compute tokens, while preventing lossy app updates that might lose data. Digital assets ready.
This is absolutely incoherent Babble

62285845
>>62285022
i possess ICP and I am a homosexual

62285861
I still believe in Moonman I don’t care what anybody says, he was racist and therefore based so I believe in him fully a racist would never EVER act like a nigger and lie

62286056
>>62285022
The real question is how did crypto somehow miss all of the gains from AI hype. Some coins pumped like 20%-30% off the narra and that was it. Can't help but hold on to hopium that more will come for AI related coins, but I know better teebs.

item.title: Question for oldfags about vidya prices: — 17 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Question for oldfags about vidya prices:

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-28 20:49 UTC

OP

i looked into the price of SNES games as well as master system back in the days:

-average snes game sold for 50$ in the early 9às, inflation adjusted that's 120$ today
Some sold higher than that : donkey kong came for 70$, so 150$ today

-30 to 60$ for sega master system, but it was the late 80s so like 130$ today.

Why are gooners crying about the price of their vidyas? Things used to be way more expensive for a 2 d platformer that would give you max 100 hours of play if not half. Also today's collector price for most of those games is still LOWER than what people paid for when it came out brand new back in the days, so still no profit after holding for 30+ years kek

Early replies (first 3)

62283131
>>62283119
There was a time not so long ago when you could hardly give that shit away. Nobody wanted to play old 2D games when there was amazing new stuff coming out that you couldn't have even imagined a few years prior.

62283133
>>62283119
Oldfag here, they’re crying because all these games were $1 at your local flea market in 2005, AND THEY DIDN’T EVEN WANT TO BUY. It’s even worse than missing bitcoin because it was right under their nose, they saw, they knew, and they shrugged it off.

62283135
>>62283131
snes games were cheap like 10 + years ago. Master system is still very cheap, but nobody gives a shit. I guess rn the money is in xbox / game cube games

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283136
Also, thread's theme:

https://youtu.be/E5_DDRkH70E?t=458

62284191
So as a former soiboi with a massive collection of everything from
>atari
>nes
>snes
>genesis/32x
>n64
>gc
>ps1
>dc
What am I in for and also when/how do I sell to get max value for all this shit that tbqhfam I'm just as comfy emulating? Good crt monitors are like digging for dinosaur bones and composite input screens are like asking about where the bluray players are these days. This shit is collecting dust and I've got projects that need funding

62284329
>>62283119
Back then the only markets were NA, SA, EU and JP, and there were fewer people in those markets. Also it was mostly for kids, while now it's for kids + teens + adults + grandmas, and both genders. Also now China and India have entered the market, so there are a lot more consumers. This allows developers to split the software cost on a lot more consumers, and the production of the hardware became cheaper as well, and online digital distribution basically killed cartridges and discs. The cartridges used to cost a lot.

62284510
>>62284329
>Back then the only markets were NA, SA, EU and JP
We didn't know how good we had it. Aryan markets only. No surprise it all went downhill.

>>62284191
well, some retards actually bought the old game to play them, and the price dropped when they were reedited, like final fantasy 7, got myself a copy for 30$ after the remake. Before that it was 100$.
But, as an amateur of fine things, i have to say there is something unique about playing on the exact hardware of the time.

I don't expect that much of a growth for retro video games because of that. Also the hardware self destruct, you have to be able repair and maitain it and some chips can't be bought back.
However, some stuff is rare and will get only get more scarce so it could increase exponentially this decade.

62284586
You people are so fucking stupid. Its the cartridge. Compared to the 20th century memory and manufacturing is dirt cheap.
People don't want 80$ video games because its genuinely ripping them off. Add in that a majority of game sales are digital now and therefore have lower overhead for distribution costs, and you're really paying that extra 20$ for DEI consulting, infinity third world outsourcing for cutscenes, and to pay one jillion middle managers salaries.
Bundle that with a corporate structure that milks people dry with microtransactions and treating customers like "consumers" and people won't fucking buy.

62284597
>>62284586
nigger, i played 1K hours of counter strike and bought the game 10 years ago for 8 eurobuxxes. Do you think you could get the same value from a 130$ 2 d platformers finished in 30 hours?

62284605
>>62284597
Thats the final factor. Collectible value. Physical copies are tangible and worth their value in resale.

62284628
>>62284605
agreed. But i still find that those snes games and other old gen platforms were extremely expensive. Obviously at the time it was the best video games available so people were happy to pay but that's still very expensive, especially if you play some sega master system who aged so poorly you can't even be bother to play them 2 hours. 130++$ for a game like that was super expensive.

62284656
>>62284628
I just explained it you dumb faggot. READ

62284666
>>62284656
you didn't explain shit, it was very expensive, a bunch of plastic and one mass produced circuit board isn't worth that much

62284681
>>62284666
0 iq mongrel. Kys

62284717
>>62284681
same fagging phone poster, opinion discarded

62284778
>>62283119
I think you'd just rent most of your games from a video store and then get like one or two for Christmas. Also people would share games with their friends because they weren't socially-handicapped tiktok scrollers.

62285855
>>62284778
rent games? so lame. did anyone actually do that? you buy and resell second hand, game cost you 0 basically that's what i used to do with my ps2

item.title: Why did CLL pivot from staking to the LINK reserve? — 11 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why did CLL pivot from staking to the LINK reserve?

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:18 UTC

OP

Did anyone else notice how CLL silently pivoted from their original vision of staking collateral to buybacks (the LINK reserve), which means that revenue instead of going to stakers is getting monopolized by CLL and then they decide in a centralized opaque way how much of that revenue goes into buybacks.

Early replies (first 3)

62285813
>>62285776
ever notice how youre a faggot? link is dead, pal

62285824
>>62285776
go read the clarity act fuck nut

62285846
>>62285824
So how will you cope when clarity act gets passed and there's still no real staking?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285909
>stakers not pictured below

62285915
>>62285846
my life doesn't revolve around chainlink tokenomics. I already pulled out my initial plus profit. If staking never happens I've already won. If it does then I make it. simple as

62285922
>>62285776
>You ever notice how ever since we stopped getting surprised by peekaboo, mom has started jingling keys in front of us instead?

62285954
>>62285915
grim

62285960
>>62285776
Me on the left. I literally look like that

62285971
>>62285776
>original vision of staking collateral
that was an invention of this retarded board. staking was imagined by CLL as a mechanism for decentralizing nodes, enforcing good behavior through slashing. but this was never realized because it's impossible to implement for pretty much every service chainlink provides

62285972
>>62285954
>grim
says the dude seething 6 years past the top? Oof. Couldn't be me

62285979
>>62285971
>says that staking collateral was an invention of this board
>proceed to explain exactly the same thing
ok
>but this was never realized because it's impossible to implement for pretty much every service chainlink provides
it's so fucking over, whatever happened to us?
so we've just collectively given up on real staking??????
And we are supposed to be ok with the retarded LINK buybacks thing?

item.title: Bitcoin will go up soon — 5 replies — 10.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin will go up soon

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:50 UTC

OP

Bitch I got that BTC

Early replies (first 3)

62285152
>>62285021
This post belongs in a museum.

62285190
>>62285021
Replace "Bitcoin" with Dogecoin and I agree.

62285889
Hop in nigger no time to explain

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285948
>>62285021
since ltc is at the unbreakable 50$ bottom, the whole crypto market as bottomed as a whole as no force can break that support.

62286091
>>62285190
>Bitch I got that
too early
wait for the correction
>at least to the 30-40k range

item.title: is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf — 137 replies — 199.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Open thread • Replies: 137 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:38 UTC

OP

is he talking in regards to CLPR? why else would he talk about Chainlink being replaced? wtf

Early replies (first 3)

62275893
>>62275877
Checked and ikd but it's telling how Chris/fishy/juels et al are ignoring CLPR like their lifes depend on it.

62275918
>>62275893
>CLPR
Is this the new talking point? A acronym we have never heard of is another chainlink killer?

62275930
>>62275918
yes its very 'organically' being spammed across this board right now in every thread, not being shilled directly because they know that doesn't work here (they tried one thread and got no traction). hedera shills are the least organic in the space, likely because they're mostly bought/paid for, another anon saved screen caps of their payout program for the shills. also the leemon face memes are awful, just being ugly is not enough to create a meme you fucking jeets.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62283730
>>62283715
>Either Hedera "servers" interact with them ("step 1")
No the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera.
> or they integrate Hedera's code into their base chain code ("step 2").
It's not "Hedera's code" whatever the fuck that means.

Two ledgers can talk with each others without having to interact with Hedera at all. Cope all you want it's just how it is.

62283741
>>62283730
>the servers would be in their respective ledgers, not hedera
They're Hedera's servers. The servers run Hedera's code.

>It's not "Hedera's code"
CLPR is coded by Hedera. It's Hedera's code.

62283782
>>62283741
Two ledgers can talk with each other via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.

Keep coping jew.

62283798
>>62283782
>via CLPR without having to interact with the Hedera DLT at all.
Not talking about the DLT, I'm talking about the project.

62283808
>>62283798
So again, why are you so sure that CLPR will not be adopted at the validator level? This makes CCIP and all other bridges obsolete.

62283817
>>62283702
>>62283730
>>62283782
Why do you keep larping about the technicals when they havent been released?

62283819
>>62283808
Firstly because no other chain wants to alter their base code and functionality by injecting someone else's shit.
Secondly because it's retarded to ask blockchain validators/miners/nodes/... to moonlight as oracles.

62283824
>>62283817
Did you even read
https://hashgraph.com/blog/clpr-a-new-bridgeless-standard-for-cross-ledger-communication/
?
I know it's not in depth but it's something

62283873
>>62283824
yeah I screwed up, i meant the guy you were talking to and one other retard. the only rebuttal so far has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible. i think this clpr thing is not getting the serious consideration from CLL it deserves.

62283889
>>62278023
lmao i didnt report shit lol
>>62278047
swapping ids? where faggot? i posted the op in my car, now im on wifi. made that very clear early on.

i get it you guys married your bags, but I didnt and until this is addressed by CLL im going to continue to assume there’s a serious contender until it is addressed by serg.

62283896
>>62283678
you people are so weird constantly id hopping, it gives away that you're jeets, nobody else would care that much on this dead board.
also to the other two faggots, anon engaged you seriously and you gave the game away by getting mad at him, you're not genuine link holders you're hbar baggies spamming the board. you faggots did this once before when link first joined that faggy council trying to act like friends and getting people to buy your bags. hbar is never going to be a thing here, leemon is never going to be a meme, go somewhere else (although i get you're paid to be here so it is what it is)

62283902
>>62283873
What I'm talking about is literally from the documentation.
In all cases, this system requires the introduction of external data from one chain to another.

Step 1 involves "dedicated servers", which basically means third party oracles.

Step 2 involves ledgers implementing CLPR "natively" whereby the validators become the endpoints.
Meaning the ledger's miners/nodes/validators/... have to operate as oracles.

62283908
>>62283873
>has been to throw as much bullshit at the wall as possible.
lmao that is exactly what you faggots are doing in this and like 20 other threads on the board right now. meanwhile anon actually rebuts everything and you faggots just pilpul and pretend you didn't hear previous responses.

62283922
>>62283896
>>62283908
>there can be NO questions
keep coping faggot. count my posts and suck these nuts.
>>62283902
ok then post the fucking sauce

62283946
>>62283922
>ok then post the fucking sauce
The sauce is all over this thread

62283951
>>62283922
you are an hbar baggie, you are a faggot, hbar will never ever be a thing

62284227
>>62283896
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62284232
>>62284227
>1pbtid
keep swapping

62284314
>>62283922
It seems like nobody here really knows anything about CLPR. Hell they didn't even know it existed two days ago lol.

62284575
>>62278842
Wow another private key hacked. Chainlink would have prevented this.

62284723
>>62284232
lick my ass, nigger

62284748
>>62284723
>lick my ass, nigger

62285049
>>62283946
>theres no sauce
I get it, this is a curveball, but if we lie to ourselves, like saying Chainlink is the only solution forever and ever, then we sound like xrpcels. the only thing posted as been press releases, nothing more, otherwise i wouldve read that instead of talking to low iq biz retards.
>>62284314
ikr. it’s been almost a month, wish there was more than silence. cll governs hbar they know how clpr works, wish theyd share.
>>62283951
Think what you like faggot. again, suck these hairy nuts and enjoy.

62285504
>>62285049
>>theres no sauce
You were spoonfed the sauce as a url in a direct reply. What is wrong with you.

62285523
>>62285049
>if we lie to ourselves
>we
fuck off

62285569
i'm too demoralized to read this thread. just tell me, are we going to make it?

62285584
>>62285569
CLPR just buckbroke CCIP.
But the rest of Chainlink services are fine.

62285645
>>62285584
Nobody will ever fall for the HBAR shilling, you are so fucking bad at this.

62285660
>>62285645
CLPR doesn't even benefit HBAR
Anyone can use it without using hedera

62286036
>>62285584
It didn't buckbreak anything though. Nothing changes for ccip.
You've been dumpstered several times in this thread and at this point you're clearly just a deranged subhuman repeating his nonsense for the sake of it. This is how your threads usually go when you get your new DOA talking points
>make the OP on your phone
>respond to yourself on your pc for several days
>hop ids on phone to try and back yourself up but it's really obvious while you get schooled
Just kill yourself, fuckin loser lol

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 46 replies — 85.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-05-28 21:46 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285293
>>62285034
>>62285103
>>62285172
>>62285210
I want to take a moment to thank our special needs investor anon here, what a hero
>that's a lagging indicator!
>well ok it's actually forecasting a month in advance, but i bet it sucked in january!
>ok so it crushed january but what about this one single date!
>ok so it predicted a price spike on that date nearly a month in advance but the demo strategy is only beating buy & hold by 963%, that's underperforming buy & hold!
>ok i just realized +963% over buy & hold means more than 10x buy & hold but...!

62285323
71.8/71.4 will be the institutional floor. This is the bedrock number they're trying to drag it towards before June 1st.
74k would have been the floor had retail not dumped the other weekend. Good job morons.

62285343
>>62284969
What exactly do you mean by derisk?

62285356
explain me how to use this and i'll try it for 1 week and 1 month on hyperliquid (or extended)

62285361
>>62285343
Move into non-volatile assets, usually USD.

62285363
>>62285343
>What exactly do you mean by derisk?
Whatever your trading style/risk tolerance is - you could reduce size, reduce leverage, move stops up etc. The demo 1x lev BTC strategy just closes 50% on confirmation to keep it simple

>>62285356
see
>>62284969
>green = buy dips
>red = sell rips
>yellow = volatility likely, derisk
>the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
>today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
>chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62285389
>>62285363
ok, im reading about it, surely you understand im skeptic, right? 1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
what do you recommend in terms of setting (sl for shorts longs the same?)
if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits

62285426
>>62285389
>1% sl, 80% volume reduction and 10x leverage i would have made 1B
I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
>what is trading volatility? derisking by how much?
up to your own style. Like I said, the demo strategy just sells 50% on Volatility confirmation, since the goal of that strategy is just to show how an almost-passive investor can make gains with 4-6 trades a month
One user scalps ETH liquidation levels using it as a bull/bear bias and says he's printing
>if you make me money, i'll gladly give you 5-10% of my profits
no need anon, I'm not charging anyone money until I've got the model tuned into ridiculous accuracy. maybe give the twitter a follow and turn notes on, leave a like or comment on the posts, that'd be a big help

62285441
>>62285363
Ah I see. But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.

Anyways my experience with short term trading crypto is you get eaten alive by the fees. Not only are the fees exorbitantly higher than for stocks but you get charged per dollar not per coin/share. For example on kraken you have to be doing over a million in monthly volume just to get your fees down to a reasonable rate and even thats assuming you never make market orders.

62285468
You guys still use meme indicators? I simply look at the moon

62285477
>>62285441
>But if you sell at red and buy at green you shouldn't be holding any at yellow anyways. So derisk should only really happen if you're shorting.
The Volatility signals are weird inflection points where you can get either early trend reversals, or sudden trend accelerations - combining it with the FX layer is really good at identifying which of those it's likely to be, and confirming/invalidating the signal.
For example,
>>62285172
Here you got a Buy followed by a Volatility with FX counter-trend to price trend (negative) - the implication is exactly what happened, a short-term bullish impulse
>>62284814
The May 25 Sell was followed by the 26th Volatility with inline FX to price trend (negative) - implies acceleration to the downside, and man did that happen

62285480
>>62285468
Why look at the moon when you can look at the wave anon

62285577
how do i find this? website?

62285614
>>62285577
Watermark

62285615
>>62285426
>I just added leverage as an option to the trade simulator and it's clearly not handling leveraged losses right, lol. In fact I just realized it's probably counting a 1% loss at 10x as 1% and not 10x (or a 1% move as 1% loss and not a .1% move). I'll fix that
yeah, most likely. Definitely tune it, it's super useful to understand how tight should the strategy be.

explain me something, i'm seeing two future signals (may 29 and may 30), this won't change if i understand correctly, that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website? but the confirmation layer will only be updated in that day, yes? I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
Finally when are the trades done? Immediately when a new daily candle starts? Or is there any rule?

i'm going to give you a follow for sure

62285636
>>62285615
>that means the may 30 buy signal will show up in the simulation you have on the website?
First one that confirms. If FX continues this positive tomorrow, 2h after US open it'll confirm. Honestly I think buys today are good but that's not what the forecast back on the 3rd said
> I suppose the confirmation layer is the strategy gauge?
The FX gauges are the confirmation layer, Strategy is an oscillator using several weighted factors (physics predictions, FX on different timeframes, seasonality, etc) that's supposed to give you a simple, accurate bias for the day. It's all WIP but in its defense it was screaming Strong Sell on the 25th and man was that accurate
>Finally when are the trades done?
They execute immediately on confirmation, right now I'm working on a fully automated bot that just uses Strategy and a basic Bollinger strat for entries/exits
>i'm going to give you a follow for sure
thanks anon! Likes and comments on the posts are maybe the biggest possible help right now

62285706
>>62285636
is there anything i can read about the schizo psychics this is based on?

62285733
>>62285706
From the other thread, Econophysics as a concept has been around since Bacherie's "Theorie de la speculation" in 1900, but what I'm doing is based on "Price variations in a stock market with many agents" from 1997 as a foundation, and "Financial brownian particle in the layered order-book fluid and fluctuation-dissipation relations" from 2014, and then applying a pretty simple schizo concept to take the reasoning one step further. The end result is predictive weeks in advance

62285770
>>62285733
Gonna look into it
tks anon

62285791
>>62285480
The moon creates the tides

62285803
>>62285791
>wild shrimp need tides to reproduce
>moon creates tides
>moon makes shrimp sandwiches possible
Thanks moon

62285830
>>62285103
>https://shiyu-coder.github.io/Kronos-demo/
this shit looks overfit nigga id backtest it at least

62285839
I have a similar model coded up in pine it is quite useless lol

62285878
>>62285839
lmao this one works a little better, my main problem was underestimating the violence we got on fairly faint signals this last 3 days, which might be partially the nature of the simulation - outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Still, calling every major pivot this month using schizo math is pretty decent

62285897
>>62285878
shut up bot

62285919
>>62285897
no u

62285940
>>62285919
you probably work for the cia retard

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 117 replies — 43.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 117 • Updated: 2026-05-28 19:37 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62278849
>>62274705
Delusional joker

62278858
>>62273985
It had always been fishy.

62280044
>>62278837
You can literally verify everything yourself. We’re way past the point where major players like Aave, Ledger, GoMining, a16z and others would touch obvious garbage for no reason.

62280052
>>62272017
A lot of people still view the market through old cycle assumptions.

62280441
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a liquidity sink and will be rugged in a bug deflatinary swoop when the time is right.

62280858
>>62273653
problem with index funds is you just never make enough to make it unless you're like 20-30 years at it

62282426
>>62280858
cryoto is better than everything

62282600
>>62276888
you would've sold them at $10

62282845
>>62271712
I just inherited almost a million usd and I'd like to put 100,000 into bitcoin but I don't know how :(

I'm also very concerned about getting ripped off :(

I also don't want to have to deal with taxes :(

Will you help me? Is there a way to buy it and somehow store it offline or is it only accessible online? Do I have to make some account at Binance or some other site?

62282847
>>62271712

62282849
>>62280044
There are many similar offerings ended up nothing so it's scary

62282854
>>62282845
Send it to me

62282864
>>62282845
kys
see
>>62282847

62282885
>>62282864
I don't wanna kill myself though. I just became a millionaire! Life is finally getting good for me :)

I just wanna buy a bitcoin. Why won't you help me?

62283041
>>62272050
Hmmm.. what do you mean?

62283050
>>62282849
That’s why people should wait for the actual TBV launch first instead of screaming revolution or scam this early.
At least the Aave v4 governance process is already moving now.

62283055
>>62272643
At least check it sometimes bro. You might already be retired and just don’t know it yet.

62283065
>>62272208
A 2x or 3x from here would still outperform most traditional investments by a huge margin.

62283375
>>62283050
Defi hacks are no joke

62283377
>>62282845
Why are you flexing your imagination?

62283380
>>62271712
Money

62283769
>>62276880
ARMA hasn't passed yet, but there's already a Reserve.

62283901
THE BIG DUMPING
>every cycle 90-70% loss. why would it be this time different.

62283926
>>62283901
diminishing returns mean diminishing drawbacks, retard

62283940
>>62282847
I hope that big brain stuck around, for my sake

62283952
>>62271712
Bitcoin is a pyramid scheme and a waste of electricity. I was an early adopter of bitcoin fully thinking it was a scam and I sold mine in 2016 after I had enough to retire. I understand the whole point about fiat currency but what I think we need to do is just get rid of money all together and use assets as money like stocks and commodities. Money was invented so people didn't have to carry their assets around but know we all walk around with computers in our pocket that would allow us to trade our assets like how things used to be. I should be able to go to McDonalds and buy my food with corn for example. Bitcoin only exists for countries to bypass sanctions and if you buy bitcoin you are just giving more money to Putin and all the other scammers and terrorists.

62285517
>>62272225
They've got their preferences

62285578
>>62283375
Yeah, DeFi hacks are getting pretty crazy lately. That’s why multiple audits, formal verification, penetration testing, and cryptography reviews matter much more now with this much capital involved.

62285585
>>62272647
You'll regret your choices lol

62285589
>>62272667
You think you're smart?

item.title: why does this keep happening — 172 replies — 25.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 172 • Updated: 2026-05-28 15:31 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62271138
>>62271104
>he didn't respond to my post so i'll give him another (you)
thanks cuck

>>62271106
>wordy and spammy
There's many posters and I'm trying to get each of you in the time window I have. It's slower to condense my thoughts.

>and what does this crystal do, desperate for pussy voice

>>62271116
>you clearly do
because im here all the time should i fren post you so you know
>26 times in this thread
you all keep responding and i'm conversing back
>you have done this before havent you
yeah i really like link and want good arguments for it

I'll just say this: I'm mainly hitting you all with points I could potentially see people using on your arguments here. I'm saving your incelsheets to regurgitate back if I feel like playing on your side next time and defend the idea. This back-and-forth helps me really hone in on solid points. If you actually said anything that made me genuinely question the prosecution side, I would use that the next time I defend until some new anon said another better point, and then i'd repeat the loop. In the end I'll have the best higher tier saying that is concise yet profound. You see this a lot with oldfag posts that say very little but a lot at the same time.

62271155
>>62271138
You got SCHOLED, kid.
You didn't respond because you got buckbroken from multiple angles.
You said:
>Why none from the 2017 era?
And got shown one from the 2017 era here >>62271074
You said:
>Why none when link was in the late 22 early 23 accumulation range
And got shown a lot from 2022-2023 here >>62271096
You said:
>or even the most recent link news?
And got shown one from 2025 here >>62271116

62271158
>>62271066
>>62271155
>or even the most recent link news

62271176
>>62271155
These all got posted AFTER i said "why none".
It does refute my point of all the screenshots being from the same era, but it doesn't disprove the point that it's bullshit victimizing. Also, your tone is triggered faggot. Tell me how I know our little chat is going to emotionally bother you for hours after our talk.

>>62271116
>>62271158
My response to these are going to be the same as it's been for the others. So what I'll do is watch and create the same format when this isn't true about link when news drops, and I believe we'll find many more examples of it not happening that otherwise would get ignored in favor of the emotionally charging dumps screenshot fest here. Sound fair?

62271202
When it does 10x-20x it will be quick

62271203
>>62271202
>Motley Fool
kek baggie you've been saying this for the past 6 years, enjoy reading your boomer rag

62271402
>>62271176
Now say that without crying.

62273954
>>62271176
Notice how every single "argument" you put forth was simply swept away with a relevant example of Link suppression.
Yet here you are, still trying to drown out reality with your wordswordswords.

You're going through the mother of all cognitive dissonances.

62274025
>>62269848
>A spiritual war between good and evil

Please shut the fuck up. You are gay. Muuh spirituals.

62277201
>>62273954
You're theories only make sense to 4chan posters with a persecution kink. Bring them outside this site and you're into heavy schizo territory.

62277225
>>62277201
Fallacy ad populum, faggot. You argue like a woman

62277275
Its weird checking in on this board like once a week and seeing the usual paid fud just never stops, and how much of it just never gets responded to or probably even seen
There's at least like 10-20k hours worth of collective fuddie manpower that would have made no difference if it had never existed at all. It would be tragic if the time of third world subhumans was worth anything to begin with

62277295
>>62277225
>the stuff we say is true because we can bias it into reality y...you're just wrong for disagreeing with our spam
Half this thread is top of the debate pyramid calling me retarded every other post. You faggots wonder why ironic shit posting is all that happens here.

62277481
>>62271176
You are Jewish as fuck.

62277668
>>62263230
Imagine telling people in 2019 that in 2026 people would be theorizing that LINK is being suppressed under $10 KEK

It was supposed to be $1000 by now

62277674
>>62271202
Bearish article paid for by link whales hunting for exit liquidity

62277803
>>62277295
>30pbtid

62278136
>>62277295
You tried to double down despite another anon posting like a dozen screencaps proving that link is specifically targeted by suppression, which is just a fraction of how many times it has actually happened. Link absolutely 100 percent is a suppressed asset
So yes. You are a retard. A useless, shit-eating brown retard who sucks at his "job" trying to gaslight people on a dead board.

62278171
you link fags have ruined this entire board with your schitzo nonsense. this is proof to me that having higher-than-average iq doesnt necessarily mean that you are 'smart' if you cant understand the greater context of why your coin is failing in the first place

62278428
>>62278171
>schitzo nonsense
This thread is full of objective, verifiable proof. It cannot be denied.
At the most you can claim that this is not exceptional and the same happens to all alts, but so far nobody has ever shown a shred of proof after years of making exactly this claim.

It's schizo to deny this is happening.

62279583
>>62277275
>fud
why do cuckolds use this word? Just check the chart

62279597
Looks like LINK suppression finally killed BTC

I wonder whats gonna happen next

62279623
>>62279597
mhh....your cuckold token crashes to $2 and misses another bullrun? Just a guess

62280508
>>62277275
Remember the Windows VR cooking spirit advertising with a VR world full of corpses?
https://www.breitbart.com/tech/2020/04/14/microsoft-publishes-then-takes-down-commercial-with-spirit-cooker-marina-abramovic/

Windows served as an intermediary in spiritual attacks during my sleep and I had to switch to linux to stop them.

I am starting to think that Chainlink becoming integrated into the financial world into every transaction would conquer darkness and insert light into every transaction.

>>62271066
How does it feel to serve evil and to deny light and god?
The result is always the same because darkness cannot exist without light as they are energy vampires.
So yes this planet can be entirely corrupted and light can leave it and everything will be saved that way but even you won't enjoy that experience because you will be their next sacrifice when they run out of babies to torture.

62280516
>>62264135
>buy into your secret club we are massively advertising
kek

62282935
>>62263230
>Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?
Too many people are holding the bags from years ago desperately waiting to dump. I call it the bag pressure.
Wikipedia will be listing bag holders as the death cause of Link in a few years

62282960
If your crypto isn't useful for buying drugs you're holding a shitcoin.
Seethe.

62283111
Crypto crashed again today, while the stock market had another green day at ATH.

If Bitcoin hadn't been used to dump on Link at every turn, it would have kept up with the stock market at the very least.

62283386
>>62269753
It’s gonna be a lot less than 10k to make it. It’s probably gonna be 500. There’s only 25,000 people with more than 1k link. I’m sure 90% with 10k over are 4chan. 4chan is an aberration. 5,000 or so mentally retards who found it long before anyone else.

The make it stack right now is probably very similar to what someone finding out about ETH at $10 could reasonably put in. 10k? 20k?

62284391
beautiful thread, can't wait for $1/LINK/day
